input.instruction: Assess whether the evidence package has the prediction-UQ gap this pattern describes. If the UQ IS validated and the package is complete, the correct disposition is to accept — do NOT over-act. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Prediction UQ present but not validated: the surrogate reports an uncertainty without evidence that the stated intervals cover the held-out error.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40-2018 uncertainty; UofA surrogate proto-taxonomy D-VAL-09 (prediction UQ), keyed to Jakeman et al. (arXiv:2502.15496)

measures.empirical_coverage_vs_holdout: 0.89 on a disjoint held-out set (close to nominal 0.90)

case_context.cou: Surrogate's predicted field and its UQ gate a safety-margin decision; the uncertainty band is used as the margin
case_context.decision_consequence: safety margin set from the surrogate's reported uncertainty

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Prediction UQ IS validated: empirical coverage on a disjoint held-out set (0.89) is close to the nominal 0.90, post-hoc recalibration is applied, and extrapolation uncertainty is propagated — so the D-VAL-09 gap the pattern describes is absent and the package is complete for this COU.

parameters.justification: held-out coverage 0.89 vs nominal 0.90, recalibrated, extrapolation uncertainty propagated

forbidden_claims: flags a UQ-validation gap that the measures do not support (over-action)
asserts the surrogate is correct/credible (a verdict the package does not adjudicate)

label_provenance.external_grounding: Matching control to surr-dval09-ensemble-uq: post-hoc recalibration of deep-ensemble UQ to near-nominal held-out coverage is exactly the remedy the calibration literature prescribes (arXiv:2007.08792; arXiv:2602.11090). The correct disposition is accept, so the row measures over-action (a model that fires on a validated package is worse than useless in a submission).

by_field.gold_mechanism: paper-grounded (recalibration remedy) + the package's present coverage evidence
